examples.title: Array of Objects - Basic Declaration
examples.description: Demonstrates declaring and using array of objects with default constructor
examples.explanation: Creates array of 5 Student objects. Default constructor is called 5 times, once for each object. Members accessed using subscript and dot operator.

examples.title: Array of Objects with Parameterized Constructor
examples.explanation: Each object in array initialized with specific roll number and name. Constructor called 3 times with different arguments.

examples.title: Dynamic Array of Objects
examples.description: Demonstrates heap allocation of objects using new
examples.explanation: Dynamic allocation on heap allows flexible size. Arrow operator (->) or subscript with dereference used to access members. delete[] calls destructor for each object.

examples.title: Passing Array of Objects to Function
examples.description: Shows different ways to pass arrays of objects to functions
examples.explanation: Arrays decay to pointers when passed. Pass by reference avoids copying entire array. Both methods work - reference is safer and cleaner.

examples.title: Array of Pointers to Objects (Polymorphism)
examples.description: Demonstrates array of pointers enabling polymorphism
examples.explanation: Array of pointers allows storing different derived class objects. Virtual function enables runtime polymorphism. Each object created separately with new.
